questions.q: In which year was the Hindi film Sholay released?
questions.explanation: Sholay was released on 15 August 1975.

questions.q: Who directed the 1975 film Sholay?
questions.explanation: Sholay was directed by Ramesh Sippy.

questions.q: Which actor played the menacing bandit Gabbar Singh in Sholay?
questions.explanation: Amjad Khan played Gabbar Singh, one of Hindi cinema's most famous villains.

questions.q: In Sholay, the character Jai was played by which actor?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan played Jai (Jaidev) in Sholay.

questions.q: Which actor played Veeru, the other hired outlaw, in Sholay?
questions.explanation: Dharmendra played Veeru in Sholay.

questions.q: The famous screenwriting duo who wrote Sholay is popularly known by which name?
questions.explanation: Sholay was written by the duo Salim-Javed (Salim Khan and Javed Akhtar).

questions.q: What is the name of the village terrorized by Gabbar Singh in Sholay?
questions.explanation: The bandit Gabbar Singh terrorizes the village of Ramgarh in Sholay.

questions.q: In Sholay, the former police officer who hires Jai and Veeru is named what?
questions.explanation: Thakur Baldev Singh, played by Sanjeev Kumar, hires the two outlaws.

questions.q: Which actress played the talkative tonga driver Basanti in Sholay?
questions.explanation: Hema Malini played the spirited tonga driver Basanti in Sholay.

questions.q: Sholay is widely described as belonging to which hybrid genre nickname?
questions.explanation: Sholay is often called a 'Curry Western', an Indian take on the Spaghetti Western style.

questions.q: Who composed the music for the 1975 film Sholay?
questions.explanation: R. D. Burman composed the music for Sholay.

questions.q: Which song from Sholay celebrates the friendship between Jai and Veeru?
questions.explanation: 'Yeh Dosti Hum Nahin Todenge' is the iconic friendship song from Sholay.

questions.q: The song 'Mehbooba Mehbooba' in Sholay was sung primarily by which artist?
questions.explanation: Music director R. D. Burman himself sang 'Mehbooba Mehbooba', picturised on Jalal Agha with Helen dancing at Gabbar's hideout.

questions.q: Which actor played the comic jailer in Sholay, delivering the line 'Hum angrezon ke zamane ke jailer hain'?
questions.explanation: Asrani played the comic jailer in Sholay.

questions.q: Which character in Sholay is associated with the catchphrase 'Arre O Samba'?
questions.explanation: Gabbar Singh calls out to his lieutenant Samba with the famous 'Arre O Samba'.

questions.q: Which actor played the comic character Soorma Bhopali in Sholay?
questions.explanation: Jagdeep played the comic character Soorma Bhopali in Sholay.

questions.q: Which actress played Radha, the Thakur's widowed daughter-in-law, in Sholay?
questions.explanation: Jaya Bhaduri played the quiet widow Radha in Sholay.

questions.q: In which rocky South Indian region was much of Sholay filmed?
questions.explanation: Sholay was largely shot among the rocky terrain of Ramanagara (then in Karnataka) near Bangalore.

questions.q: Who wrote the lyrics for the songs of Sholay, including 'Yeh Dosti' and 'Mehbooba Mehbooba'?
questions.explanation: Anand Bakshi wrote the lyrics for the songs of Sholay, set to R. D. Burman's music.

questions.q: How are the characters Jai and Veeru introduced at the start of Sholay?
options: As petty criminals and small-time crooks
questions.explanation: Jai and Veeru are small-time crooks whom the Thakur recruits to capture Gabbar Singh.

questions.q: Which production banner produced the 1975 film Sholay?
questions.explanation: Sholay was produced under the Sippy Films banner, with G. P. Sippy as producer.

questions.q: What punishment does Gabbar Singh inflict on the Thakur that drives the Thakur's quest for revenge in Sholay?
questions.explanation: Gabbar Singh cuts off both of the Thakur's arms, which is why he hides them under a shawl.

questions.q: Initially the censors required Sholay's original ending to be changed; what was the altered outcome for Gabbar Singh?
options: Gabbar is handed over to the police alive instead of being killed by the Thakur
questions.explanation: The censors objected to the Thakur killing Gabbar, so the released ending has the police arrive and take Gabbar away.

questions.q: Sholay famously had a long unbroken theatrical run at which Mumbai cinema?
questions.explanation: Sholay ran continuously for over five years at the Minerva theatre in Mumbai.

questions.q: Which actor was originally considered for the role of Gabbar Singh before Amjad Khan was cast in Sholay?
questions.explanation: Danny Denzongpa was first considered for Gabbar but could not do it due to a scheduling conflict, and Amjad Khan was cast.

questions.q: Who served as the cinematographer for Sholay, helping achieve its expansive widescreen look?
questions.explanation: Dwarka Divecha was the cinematographer of Sholay.

questions.q: The character Samba, Gabbar's lieutenant in Sholay, was played by which actor?
questions.explanation: Mac Mohan played the largely silent dacoit Samba in Sholay.

questions.q: Which actor played Kaalia, the dacoit famously taunted by Gabbar in the 'Kitne aadmi the' scene in Sholay?
questions.explanation: Viju Khote played Kaalia, the dacoit Gabbar interrogates in the iconic 'Kitne aadmi the' scene.

questions.q: A re-released 3D version of Sholay was brought to cinemas in which year?
questions.explanation: A converted 3D version of Sholay was released in cinemas in 2014.

questions.q: Although hugely successful overall, how did Sholay perform with critics and at the box office during its very first weeks of release?
options: It was an instant unanimous hit from day one
It was banned for several weeks
It was pulled after one week
It opened slowly and received lukewarm early reviews before becoming a massive long-run success
questions.explanation: Sholay had a slow start with cool early reviews but went on to become one of Indian cinema's biggest long-running successes.
